report_name: ECB Orders Banks to Submit AI Cyber-Defense Plans by October 31

identities.name: European Central Bank
identities.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) has ordered all major banks under its supervision to create and submit formal action plans detailing how they will mitigate cyber risks from advanced AI models.

identities.name: European Systemic Risk Board
identities.description: Voiced concern that advanced AI now represents a systemic risk to the financial sector, a concern which is a primary driver for the ECB's directive.

identities.name: J.P. Morgan
identities.description: A U.S. firm with euro-area subsidiaries that is subject to the ECB's mandate to submit an AI cyber risk action plan.

identities.name: Goldman Sachs
identities.description: A U.S. firm with euro-area subsidiaries that is subject to the ECB's mandate to submit an AI cyber risk action plan.

identities.name: Citi
identities.description: A U.S. firm with euro-area subsidiaries that is subject to the ECB's mandate to submit an AI cyber risk action plan.

identities.name: Morgan Stanley
identities.description: A U.S. firm with euro-area subsidiaries that is subject to the ECB's mandate to submit an AI cyber risk action plan.

identities.name: Banks supervised by the ECB
identities.description: The group of financial institutions directly supervised by the ECB who are mandated to submit AI cyber risk action plans by October 31, 2026.
